title: Ecosystem-update + reapply-sweep phases (improver Fitting)

review.summary: Fixed: unguarded persistence writes violating 'never throws' contract, duplicated marker literal, 3x duplicated log/state-dir boilerplate, apm error-classification bug, reapply-sweep reason-mislabeling, missing UI Run Now wiring, em-dash CLAUDE.md violations. Logged 2 architectural findings to docs/decisions.md (composition-dir env-var gap, review-queue.json locking gap).

overrides: review-queue.json has no locking between server.mjs and cron-fired improver.mjs - already logged docs/decisions.md 2026-07-01 'review-queue.json has no locking...'; pre-existing hazard broadened slightly by this slice, needs a lock/CAS fix across 3 files, out of scope for a single slice.

json-log.mjs silently drops prior history on corruption - consistent with every other tolerant-JSON-load helper already in this codebase (loadQueue, loadAutonomy, provenance.mjs readLock); lower severity than the queue fix since these are audit-trail logs, not operational state. Codex agreed this is reasonable to defer (round 2).

codexPwTest.notes: Codex's own isolated fixture verification (its throwaway Node script exercising runEcosystemUpdate and runReapplySweep directly, plus source-inspection confirming both phases are wired before the IMPROVER_PROJECTS_DIR branch in improver.mjs and before the dream logic in server.mjs) PASSED cleanly - matched all acceptance criteria including the malformed-queue no-clobber fix. Its only failing leg was a real `node improver.mjs run-now` invocation against the default ~/.garrison state dir, which hit EPERM - but Codex's own sandbox banner confirms it runs `workspace-write [workdir, /tmp, $TMPDIR]`, and ~/.garrison is outside that whitelist. Verified independently (outside Codex's sandbox): `IMPROVER_DATA=<temp dir> node fittings/seed/improver/scripts/improver.mjs run-now` exits 0, writes both ecosystem-update-log.json and reapply-sweep-log.json correctly, and the apm.yml-absence guard fires as designed. This is the same class of environment restriction the skill's socket-bind EPERM carve-out describes (sandbox blocks a path outside its whitelist), not a product defect - not sent back to autothing-implement.

notes: Round-1 Codex finding (high, 0.98 confidence): runReapplySweep unconditionally called saveQueue() even when loadQueue's tolerant catch-all returned [] for an unreadable/malformed file, which would have clobbered the real queue. Fixed via a local probeQueue() that distinguishes 'no file yet' from 'unreadable/malformed', plus a mutated-tracking guard so saveQueue only runs on a real change. Round 2: clean approve, no remaining findings.
